fact_updates.rationale: Two consecutive supply_low events occurred at 14 and 15 days since last order, both for 128-unit reorders of size 2 diapers. This suggests a consistent ~14-day reorder cycle.
Both supply_low events explicitly reference 'size 2 diapers' as the item being reordered.
Both reorder events used the same quantity of 128 units, and both were approved, indicating this is the established reorder quantity.
128 units over ~14 days yields approximately 9.1 diapers per day, consistent with typical infant consumption and supported by the observed reorder cycle.
Both place_supply_order actions were approved without rejection, suggesting the family consistently approves supply reorders.

episode_summaries.summary: Size 2 diaper supply ran low after approximately 14 days. A reorder of 128 units was placed and approved.
Size 2 diaper supply ran low again after approximately 15 days. A second reorder of 128 units was placed and approved, confirming a consistent ~14-day replenishment cycle.

pattern_detections.pattern: Diaper supply runs low on a consistent ~14-day cycle, triggering a 128-unit reorder of size 2 diapers.
pattern_detections.support: Two consecutive supply_low events at 14 and 15 days since last order, both with identical reorder quantity, both approved.

pattern_detections.pattern: Family reliably approves diaper supply reorders without rejection.
pattern_detections.support: Both place_supply_order actions in the observation window resulted in 'approved' outcomes.
